story_assessments.story_title: AI is being judged by work, not demos
story_assessments.target_audience: Knowledge workers, team leads, and AI adopters who need to decide whether automation is actually worth trusting.
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want a practical way to tell whether AI is really saving time and reducing risk, instead of just producing fast-looking output.
story_assessments.interest_signal: A measurement shift: useful work completed, cost per successful task, dependability, and value at scale.
story_assessments.stop_reason: It challenges the common assumption that a quick draft is automatically a good workflow.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A simple way to judge AI by retries, edits, cleanup, and human review before expanding a workflow.
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI’s scorecard says AI value should be measured by useful work completed, cost per successful task, dependability, and whether value improves at scale.
story_assessments.score_reason: Broad, concrete, and easy to explain fast. It gives a usable test for everyday AI use and fits both workers and managers.

story_assessments.story_title: Consumer AI is moving toward age prediction and family controls
story_assessments.target_audience: Parents, families, educators, and anyone using consumer AI on shared devices.
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want clearer safety settings and a way to manage AI use before a problem appears.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Consumer AI is starting to behave like household software with built-in age gating and parent controls.
story_assessments.stop_reason: Age prediction and stronger safeguards make safety a product feature, not an optional add-on.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: What to review in ChatGPT settings now, especially in shared-device or family environments.
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI said it is rolling out age prediction on ChatGPT consumer plans and pointed to parent controls such as quiet hours, memory settings, and distress notifications.
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong household relevance and clear safety implications, though narrower than the measurement story.

story_assessments.story_title: Google is turning notebook research into a connected workflow
story_assessments.target_audience: Researchers, analysts, knowledge workers, and security-minded teams using AI workspaces.
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to use more capable AI without exposing sensitive data or losing control over permissions.
story_assessments.interest_signal: A research tool now includes code execution, data analysis, and syncing across products.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The tool is no longer just a scratch pad; it can act on data and connect across surfaces.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A practical boundary-setting check for what data belongs in a connected AI workspace.
story_assessments.proof_point: Google renamed NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, added a secure cloud computer for code execution and data analysis, and said it will sync across Gemini and Search.
story_assessments.score_reason: Useful for anyone handling notes, analysis, or sensitive material in AI workspaces, with a clear operational takeaway.

story_assessments.story_title: EU rules are starting to shape the AI distribution layer
story_assessments.target_audience: AI builders, product leaders, platform strategists, and compliance teams.
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need a fallback plan for access, regional rules, and platform dependence.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Regulation is starting to shape which AI products can reach devices and search surfaces.
story_assessments.stop_reason: Distribution and access are becoming governance issues, not just growth issues.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A reminder to plan for platform access, compliance, and fallback options before launch.
story_assessments.proof_point: Reuters reported that EU regulators said Google must open certain Android features to AI rivals and share some search-optimisation data with AI chatbots, with changes starting in January and some user-facing changes from July 2027.
story_assessments.score_reason: Niche but important for builders and operators. The compliance and fallback-planning angle is strong and timely.

tiktok.story_title: AI is being judged by work, not demos
tiktok.target_audience: Knowledge workers and everyday AI users who want a fast practical way to judge whether automation is working.
tiktok.format: short spoken hook plus one concrete test
tiktok.opening: Is your AI saving time or creating cleanup?
tiktok.payoff: OpenAI says AI should be judged by useful work completed, cost per successful task, and dependability.
tiktok.selection_reason: It is broad, immediate, and easy to compress into one sharp question with a simple either/or comment prompt.

youtube.story_title: AI is being judged by work, not demos
youtube.target_audience: Search-driven viewers who want a practical framework for judging AI workflows.
youtube.format: explainer short with a clear test and takeaway
youtube.opening: Is your AI workflow actually saving time?
youtube.payoff: Use OpenAI’s scorecard idea to measure useful work completed, retries, edits, and human cleanup before expanding a workflow.
youtube.selection_reason: It supports a clearer mini-explanation than the other stories and gives viewers a repeatable test they can try right away.

facebook.story_title: Consumer AI is moving toward age prediction and family controls
facebook.target_audience: Parents, families, and shared-device users who need a straightforward safety update.
facebook.opening: Would you check your ChatGPT settings today?
facebook.payoff: OpenAI is rolling out age prediction and stronger safeguards, plus controls like quiet hours, memory settings, and distress notifications.
facebook.selection_reason: This is the most recognizable family and household scenario, so it should invite practical comments instead of abstract debate.

pinterest.story_title: Google is turning notebook research into a connected workflow
pinterest.target_audience: People saving practical guides on AI workflows, research tools, and data-safety checklists.
pinterest.opening: Checklist before you upload notes to Gemini Notebook
pinterest.payoff: A simple boundary-setting checklist helps you decide what data can go into a connected AI workspace, who can approve it, and what should stay human-controlled.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest rewards saveable guides, and this story naturally becomes a checklist around connected-workspace safety.

linkedin.story_title: EU rules are starting to shape the AI distribution layer
linkedin.target_audience: AI product leaders, operators, and compliance-minded professionals.
linkedin.opening: Would your AI launch survive a platform rule change?
linkedin.payoff: EU-driven access changes make distribution a governance issue, so teams need fallback plans for access, regional compliance, and platform dependence.
linkedin.selection_reason: It is the strongest professional and operational angle, with clear implications for launch planning and dependency management.

overall_reasoning: This week’s strongest audience fit is the shift from capability to control: measurement, safeguards, connected workflows, and distribution risk. Story 0 is the broadest and most reusable across general audiences, story 1 is the clearest household safety angle, story 2 is the best workflow-and-permissions story for professionals, and story 3 is the sharpest strategic/compliance angle. The platform picks separate those interests instead of forcing one story everywhere.
